title: aptamer_oligonucleotide_cross_v1 — CROSS-AXIS APTAMER x OLIGONUCLEOTIDE (shared nucleic-acid chemistry, opposite mechanisms)
description: Output schema for _python_bridge/module/aptamer_oligonucleotide_cross.py. A CROSS-AXIS in-silico side-by-side of two nucleic-acid therapeutic modalities that share the same backbone chemistry and the same nearest-neighbor (SantaLucia 1998 PNAS 95:1460) thermodynamic real-limit anchor but emit MECHANISTICALLY OPPOSITE model signals: (a) the OLIGONUCLEOTIDE / ASO path hybridizes a target mRNA via Watson-Crick complementarity and is sequence-driven; its model signal is a duplex hybridization Tm in Celsius (and the duplex dG37 / dH / dS). (b) the APTAMER path folds an oligonucleotide into a 3D pocket that recognizes a non-nucleic-acid ligand and is shape-driven, with k_cat=0; its model signal is the target-binding Kd in nM (and the fold dG / Langmuir saturation). CRITICAL HONESTY: this is a MODALITY COMPARISON of two in-silico model signatures, NOT an efficacy ranking and NOT a claim that one modality is superior. Both modalities have independent FDA-approved drug precedents (nusinersen/Spinraza 2016, patisiran/Onpattro 2018, inclisiran 2021 for OLIGONUCLEOTIDE; pegaptanib/Macugen 2004, avacincaptad pegol/Izervay 2023 for APTAMER) and each modality is described ONLY via its own precedent (g3/f1/f_lattice_fit). The two model signals live in different units on different mechanisms (hybridization Tm in C vs target-binding Kd in nM) and are structurally non-rankable: signals_commensurable=false. Real-limit anchor (g1): SantaLucia 1998 unified nearest-neighbor model is the SHARED thermodynamic backbone (both modalities rest on it); the aptamer Kd anchor is Bock 1992 Nature 355:564 (thrombin-binding DNA aptamer, low/mid-nM regime). No fork (f3): the two mechanism models are imported from aptamer_affinity_sim.py and oligonucleotide_hybridization_sim.py. In-silico simulator-consistency only (g8/f2); CROSS != new axis (core-5 axis set unchanged).

shared_chemistry.description: The shared chemistry of the two modalities (nucleic-acid backbone; Watson-Crick / wobble pairing alphabet).

shared_thermodynamic_anchor.description: The shared real-limit anchor — the SantaLucia (1998) unified nearest-neighbor nucleic-acid thermodynamic model governs both modalities (hybridization duplex stability for ASO; folded-stem stack free energy for aptamer).

shared_anchor_citation.description: Literature citation for the shared real-limit anchor (SantaLucia 1998 PNAS 95:1460).

modality_rows.description: Exactly two modality signature rows: OLIGONUCLEOTIDE (ASO/siRNA, sequence-driven hybridization) and APTAMER (shape-driven folded binder).

unification_row.description: Unification row showing the SHARED thermodynamic anchor and the MECHANISM-DISTINCT outputs (cross != merge: same backbone, opposite modality).

mechanism_distinction.description: Prose summary of the opposite-mechanism distinction (sequence-driven hybridization vs shape-driven fold-binding).

aptamer_kcat_per_s.description: Aptamers are NON-catalytic binders by definition; k_cat MUST be exactly 0.

comparison_is_ranking.description: MUST be false. This deliverable is a descriptive side-by-side, never a ranking of the modalities.

comparison_kind.description: Human-readable description of the comparison kind (descriptive side-by-side; same chemistry, opposite mechanism).

signals_commensurable.description: MUST be false. The two model signals are in different units on different mechanisms (hybridization Tm in C vs target-binding Kd in nM) and cannot be ranked against each other.

signals_commensurable_note.description: Explanation of why the two signals are non-commensurable despite sharing the SantaLucia NN backbone.

honesty.description: Mandatory honesty block — comparison is NOT a ranking and NOT a clinical/efficacy/superiority claim (g8/f2). Both modalities have own FDA-approved precedent.

statement.description: Prose honesty statement asserting comparison != ranking and in-silico-only scope; describes modalities only via their own FDA-approved drug precedent (g3/f1).

modality_row.description: One modality's in-silico model signature. The model_signal is anchored to that modality's OWN real limit (which here shares the SantaLucia 1998 NN backbone for both) and described via that modality's OWN drug precedent — never lattice-derived.

modality.description: Which of the two nucleic-acid modalities this row describes.

axis_module.description: The imported axis module that produced the model signal (no fork — governance f3).

drug_precedent.description: Own FDA-approved drug precedent for the modality (g3/f1 — described by precedent, never lattice-derived).

mechanism.description: How the modality acts (Watson-Crick hybridization vs folded-pocket recognition).

mechanism_kind.description: Discriminator distinguishing the two opposite mechanisms despite shared chemistry.

model_signal_name.description: Name of the in-silico model-computed signal for this modality.

model_signal_value.description: The model-computed signal value. NOT comparable across modalities — different units, different mechanisms.

model_signal_units.description: Units / interpretation of model_signal_value (Celsius / nM / etc.).

model_signal_detail.description: Modality-specific breakdown of the model signal (duplex NN thermodynamics + Tm for OLIGONUCLEOTIDE; fold ΔG + Langmuir Kd / theta isotherm for APTAMER).

real_limit_anchor.description: The real physical/thermodynamic limit this modality's signal is anchored to (g1 — real-limits-first).

real_limit_citations.description: Literature citations for the modality's real-limit anchor.

kcat_per_s.description: Catalytic turnover. APTAMER rows MUST report 0.0 (non-catalytic binder). OLIGONUCLEOTIDE may omit this field (sequence-driven hybridization is not framed as catalysis).

in_silico_only.description: MUST be true — in-silico simulator-consistency scope only (g8/f2).
